**PLANT TRAIT EXPRESSION PLATFORM LICENSE AGREEMENT**

**(TraitForge)**

This Plant Trait Expression Platform License Agreement (this \"**Agreement**\") is entered into as of January 1, 2018 (the \"**Effective Date**\"), by and between:

**NOVATRAIT GENOMICS CORPORATION**, a Massachusetts corporation with its principal place of business at 77 Kendall Square, Building 6, Cambridge, MA 02142 (\"**Licensor**\" or \"**NovaTrait**\"),

and

**GREENFIELD BIOSCIENCES, INC.**, a Delaware corporation with its principal place of business at 4200 Research Park Drive, Suite 310, Raleigh, NC 27609 (\"**Licensee**\" or \"**Greenfield**\").

NovaTrait and Greenfield are each referred to herein individually as a \"**Party**\" and collectively as the \"**Parties**.\"

**RECITALS**

**WHEREAS**, NovaTrait has developed and owns certain proprietary plant trait expression technologies, including patented expression vectors, biological materials, and associated know-how, marketed under the trade name \"TraitForge\" (as further defined herein);

**WHEREAS**, Greenfield is engaged in the development and commercialization of genetically enhanced crop varieties for the agricultural market and desires to obtain a license under NovaTrait\'s proprietary TraitForge platform for the purpose of expressing certain commercially valuable plant traits in specified crop species;

**WHEREAS**, the Parties desire to set forth the terms and conditions under which NovaTrait shall grant to Greenfield, and Greenfield shall accept, a license to use the Licensed IP (as defined below) within the Field of Use and Territory defined herein;

**WHEREAS**, the Parties have negotiated this Agreement at arm\'s length, with the benefit of independent legal counsel, and each Party has had adequate opportunity to review and evaluate the terms set forth herein;

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants, agreements, representations, and warranties set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

**[ARTICLE I --- DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

**1.1** \"**Affiliate**\" means, with respect to any Party, any entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with such Party, where \"control\" means the ownership or control, directly or indirectly, of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting securities or comparable equity interests of such entity.

**1.2** \"**Biological Materials**\" means the proprietary expression vectors, plasmids, transformation cassettes, cell lines, seed stocks, and other biological or genetic materials provided by NovaTrait to Greenfield pursuant to this Agreement and identified in **Exhibit B** attached hereto, as such exhibit may be amended from time to time by mutual written agreement of the Parties.

**1.3** \"**Confidential Information**\" means all non-public information, whether written, oral, visual, electronic, or in any other form, disclosed by or on behalf of one Party (the \"**Disclosing Party**\") to the other Party (the \"**Receiving Party**\") in connection with this Agreement, including without limitation trade secrets, know-how, technical data, business plans, financial information, customer lists, and any information designated as \"confidential\" or \"proprietary\" at the time of disclosure or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure.

**1.4** \"**Effective Date**\" has the meaning set forth in the preamble.

**1.5** \"**Field of Use**\" means the expression of drought tolerance and nutrient uptake efficiency traits in corn (*Zea mays*), soybean (*Glycine max*), and cotton (*Gossypium* spp.) for agricultural and commercial purposes.

**1.6** \"**Good Manufacturing Practices**\" or \"**GMP**\" means the current good manufacturing practices as promulgated by the applicable Regulatory Authority.

**1.7** \"**Improvement**\" means any modification, enhancement, derivative work, adaptation, or other change to the Licensed IP or Biological Materials, whether patentable or not, that is conceived, developed, or reduced to practice by or on behalf of either Party during the Term.

**1.8** \"**Licensed IP**\" means, collectively: (a) the Licensed Patents; (b) the Biological Materials; (c) all know-how, trade secrets, and technical information owned or controlled by NovaTrait that is reasonably necessary for Greenfield to practice the Licensed Patents and utilize the Biological Materials within the Field of Use, as described in **Exhibit A** attached hereto; and (d) any NovaTrait Improvements thereto made during the Term.

**1.9** \"**Licensed Patents**\" means: (a) United States Patent No. 10,443,076; (b) United States Patent No. 10,443,077; (c) United States Patent No. 10,889,201; and (d) all corresponding foreign patent applications and patents filed in the European Patent Office (\"**EP**\") and the Brazilian Patent Office (\"**INPI**\"), together with all continuations, continuations-in-part, divisionals, reissues, re-examinations, and extensions thereof, each as listed in **Exhibit A**.

**1.10** \"**Licensed Products**\" means any product, composition, variety, or progeny thereof: (a) the development, manufacture, use, sale, offer for sale, or importation of which would, but for the license granted herein, infringe one or more Valid Claims of the Licensed Patents; or (b) that is developed, manufactured, or produced using, incorporating, or derived from the Biological Materials or the know-how included in the Licensed IP.

**1.11** \"**Net Sales**\" means the gross amounts invoiced or received by Greenfield, its Affiliates, or any authorized sublicensees for the sale, lease, or other disposition of Licensed Products to Third Parties in bona fide arm\'s-length transactions, less the following deductions to the extent actually incurred, allowed, or accrued in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles consistently applied:

> \(a\) trade, cash, and quantity discounts actually granted to purchasers;
>
> \(b\) credits, allowances, and refunds for returns of Licensed Products;
>
> \(c\) freight, shipping, transportation, and insurance charges related to the delivery of Licensed Products to the extent separately identified on invoices or otherwise documented;
>
> \(d\) sales, use, value-added, excise, and other similar taxes, tariffs, and governmental charges imposed on the sale of Licensed Products to the extent included in gross invoiced amounts and borne by Greenfield or its Affiliate (but excluding income taxes);
>
> \(e\) rebates and charge-backs paid or credited to managed care organizations, group purchasing organizations, government agencies, and similar entities; and
>
> \(f\) amounts written off as uncollectible in accordance with Greenfield\'s standard accounting practices, consistently applied, provided that if any such amounts are subsequently collected, they shall be included in Net Sales for the period in which collected.

In no event shall any particular amount be deducted more than once in calculating Net Sales. Sales or transfers of Licensed Products between or among Greenfield and its Affiliates for resale shall not be included in Net Sales, but the subsequent resale to a Third Party shall be included. Sales or dispositions of Licensed Products for use in research, development, clinical trials, regulatory submissions, or as promotional samples without charge shall not be included in Net Sales.

**1.12** \"**NovaTrait Improvement**\" means any Improvement conceived, developed, or reduced to practice solely by NovaTrait or its employees, agents, or contractors during the Term.

**1.13** \"**Greenfield Improvement**\" means any Improvement conceived, developed, or reduced to practice solely by Greenfield or its employees, agents, or contractors during the Term.

**1.14** \"**Joint Improvement**\" means any Improvement conceived, developed, or reduced to practice jointly by employees, agents, or contractors of both Parties during the Term.

**1.15** \"**Regulatory Authority**\" means any national, regional, state, or local governmental or regulatory body, agency, department, or authority with jurisdiction over the development, manufacture, commercialization, or sale of Licensed Products, including without limitation the United States Department of Agriculture (\"**USDA**\"), the United States Environmental Protection Agency (\"**EPA**\"), the United States Food and Drug Administration (\"**FDA**\"), and their foreign equivalents.

**1.16** \"**Royalty Term**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 4.1.

**1.17** \"**Sublicensee**\" means a Third Party to whom Greenfield has granted a sublicense in accordance with Section 3.3.

**1.18** \"**Term**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 12.1.

**1.19** \"**Territory**\" means the Western Hemisphere, defined as the geographic area comprising North America (including the United States of America, Canada, and Mexico), Central America, the Caribbean, and South America.

**1.20** \"**Third Party**\" means any person or entity other than NovaTrait, Greenfield, or their respective Affiliates.

**1.21** \"**Valid Claim**\" means a claim of any issued and unexpired Licensed Patent that has not been held unenforceable or invalid by a final, unappealable decision of a court or governmental agency of competent jurisdiction, and that has not been admitted to be invalid or unenforceable through reissue, re-examination, disclaimer, or otherwise.

**[ARTICLE II --- REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES]{.underline}**

**2.1** **Mutual Representations.** Each Party represents and warrants to the other that, as of the Effective Date:

> \(a\) it is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of its jurisdiction of incorporation;
>
> \(b\) it has the corporate power and authority to execute, deliver, and perform its obligations under this Agreement;
>
> \(c\) the execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement have been duly authorized by all necessary corporate action;
>
> \(d\) this Agreement constitutes a valid and binding obligation of such Party, enforceable against it in accordance with its terms, subject to applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium, and similar laws affecting creditors\' rights generally and to general principles of equity; and
>
> \(e\) the execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement do not and will not conflict with, violate, or result in a breach of any provision of such Party\'s organizational documents, any applicable law, or any agreement or instrument to which such Party is a party or by which it is bound.

**2.2** **NovaTrait Representations.** NovaTrait further represents and warrants to Greenfield that, as of the Effective Date:

> \(a\) NovaTrait is the sole and exclusive owner of the Licensed Patents and the Biological Materials, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, and security interests, except as disclosed in **Exhibit C**;
>
> \(b\) the Licensed Patents identified in **Exhibit A** are subsisting and, to NovaTrait\'s knowledge, are valid and enforceable;
>
> \(c\) NovaTrait has the right to grant the licenses and rights granted herein without the consent of any Third Party;
>
> \(d\) to NovaTrait\'s knowledge, the practice of the Licensed Patents and the use of the Biological Materials within the Field of Use and Territory does not infringe the intellectual property rights of any Third Party; and
>
> \(e\) to NovaTrait\'s knowledge, no Third Party is infringing any of the Licensed Patents as of the Effective Date.

**2.3** **Disclaimer.** EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY SET FORTH IN THIS ARTICLE II, NEITHER PARTY MAKES ANY REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, STATUTORY, OR OTHERWISE, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION ANY WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, NON-INFRINGEMENT, OR TITLE, AND EACH PARTY HEREBY DISCLAIMS ALL SUCH WARRANTIES.

**[ARTICLE III --- LICENSE GRANT]{.underline}**

**3.1** **License Grant.** Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the payment of all amounts due hereunder, NovaTrait hereby grants to Greenfield a non-exclusive (except as set forth in Section 3.2), non-transferable (except as set forth in Section 15.5) license under the Licensed IP to develop, make, have made, use, sell, offer for sale, import, and otherwise commercialize Licensed Products within the Field of Use and within the Territory during the Term.

**3.2** **Exclusivity.** The license granted under Section 3.1 shall be exclusive within the Field of Use and the Territory during the first three (3) years following the Effective Date (the \"**Exclusivity Period**\"), subject to NovaTrait\'s retained right to practice the Licensed IP for its own internal research and development purposes (but not for commercial sale of Licensed Products within the Field of Use and Territory). Following the Exclusivity Period, the license shall convert to a non-exclusive license for the remainder of the Term. For the avoidance of doubt, during the Exclusivity Period, NovaTrait shall not grant any other license under the Licensed IP to any Third Party for the development or commercialization of Licensed Products within the Field of Use and the Territory.

**3.3** **Sublicensing.** Greenfield shall not grant any sublicense under the rights licensed hereunder without the prior written consent of NovaTrait. Any request for consent to sublicense shall be submitted in writing to NovaTrait not less than sixty (60) days prior to the proposed effective date of such sublicense and shall include: (a) the identity of the proposed Sublicensee; (b) a summary of the proposed sublicense terms; (c) the scope of rights to be sublicensed; and (d) the business rationale for the proposed sublicense. NovaTrait shall respond to any such request within thirty (30) days of receipt. In the event that NovaTrait grants consent, Greenfield shall provide NovaTrait with a complete copy of the executed sublicense agreement within fifteen (15) days of execution. Each sublicense granted hereunder shall be subject to and consistent with the terms and conditions of this Agreement, and Greenfield shall remain primarily liable to NovaTrait for all obligations of any Sublicensee as if such obligations were the obligations of Greenfield directly.

**3.4** **No Implied Licenses.** Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to grant to either Party any right or license under any intellectual property rights of the other Party, whether by implication, estoppel, or otherwise, except as expressly set forth in this Article III.

**3.5** **Reservation of Rights.** NovaTrait reserves all rights not expressly granted to Greenfield hereunder. Without limiting the foregoing, NovaTrait retains the right to practice and license the Licensed IP outside the Field of Use and outside the Territory without restriction.

**[ARTICLE IV --- ROYALTIES AND PAYMENTS]{.underline}**

**4.1** **Royalty Rate.** In consideration of the license granted hereunder, Greenfield shall pay to NovaTrait a running royalty equal to three and three-quarters percent (3.75%) of Net Sales of Licensed Products during the Term (the \"**Royalty Term**\"). The royalty obligation under this Section 4.1 shall apply on a Licensed Product-by-Licensed Product and country-by-country basis for so long as a Valid Claim of a Licensed Patent covers such Licensed Product in such country during the Term.

**4.2** **Minimum Annual Royalty.** Commencing with the first full calendar year following the Effective Date (i.e., calendar year 2019), and for each calendar year thereafter during the Royalty Term, Greenfield shall pay to NovaTrait a minimum annual royalty of Two Million Five Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$2,500,000) (the \"**Minimum Annual Royalty**\"). If the running royalties payable under Section 4.1 for any calendar year are less than the Minimum Annual Royalty, Greenfield shall pay the difference between the running royalties actually paid and the Minimum Annual Royalty within sixty (60) days following the end of such calendar year. For the calendar year 2018, the Minimum Annual Royalty shall be prorated on a daily basis.

**4.3** **Payment Terms.** Running royalties under Section 4.1 shall be calculated and paid on a quarterly basis within forty-five (45) days following the end of each calendar quarter (i.e., by February 14, May 15, August 14, and November 14 of each year). Each quarterly royalty payment shall be accompanied by a written report (a \"**Royalty Report**\") setting forth in reasonable detail, on a country-by-country and Licensed Product-by-Licensed Product basis: (a) the gross amounts invoiced for Licensed Products during the applicable quarter; (b) the deductions applied pursuant to Section 1.11 to calculate Net Sales; (c) the resulting Net Sales; and (d) the royalty due thereon. Royalty Reports shall be treated as Confidential Information of Greenfield.

**4.4** **Currency; Exchange Rate.** All payments under this Agreement shall be made in United States Dollars. For sales of Licensed Products invoiced in a currency other than United States Dollars, the applicable Net Sales shall be converted to United States Dollars using the average exchange rate for the applicable calendar quarter as published by the Federal Reserve Board.

**4.5** **Late Payments.** Any amount not paid when due under this Agreement shall bear interest from the date due until paid at a rate equal to the lesser of (a) one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month, compounded monthly, and (b) the maximum rate permitted by applicable law.

**4.6** **Taxes and Withholding.** All payments under this Agreement are exclusive of any taxes, levies, duties, or similar governmental charges. If Greenfield is required by applicable law to withhold or deduct any taxes from payments due to NovaTrait hereunder, Greenfield shall: (a) deduct such taxes from the payment; (b) pay the taxes to the appropriate governmental authority in a timely manner; and (c) promptly provide NovaTrait with official receipts or other documentation evidencing the payment of such taxes. The Parties shall cooperate in good faith to minimize any withholding taxes to the extent permitted by applicable law.

**[ARTICLE V --- RECORDS AND AUDIT RIGHTS]{.underline}**

**5.1** **Books and Records.** Greenfield shall keep and maintain, and shall require its Affiliates and Sublicensees to keep and maintain, complete and accurate books and records relating to Net Sales and the calculation of royalties due under this Agreement, in sufficient detail to permit verification of the accuracy of all Royalty Reports and payments. Such books and records shall be maintained for a period of not less than five (5) years following the end of the calendar year to which they pertain.

**5.2** **Audit Rights.** NovaTrait shall have the right, at its own expense (subject to Section 5.3), to cause an independent, nationally recognized certified public accounting firm reasonably acceptable to Greenfield (the \"**Auditor**\") to inspect and audit the books and records of Greenfield and its Affiliates relating to Net Sales and royalties during normal business hours, not more than once per calendar year, upon not less than thirty (30) calendar days\' prior written notice to Greenfield. Such audit shall be limited to the books and records pertaining to the three (3) calendar years immediately preceding the date of the audit request. Greenfield shall make all relevant books and records available to the Auditor at Greenfield\'s principal place of business or such other location where such records are maintained in the ordinary course of business.

**5.3** **Audit Costs and Adjustments.** If any audit conducted pursuant to Section 5.2 reveals an underpayment of royalties of more than five percent (5%) of the total royalties due for the period audited, Greenfield shall bear the reasonable costs of such audit. Any underpayment revealed by an audit shall be paid by Greenfield to NovaTrait within thirty (30) days following Greenfield\'s receipt of the Auditor\'s written report, together with interest thereon as provided in Section 4.5. Any overpayment revealed by an audit shall be credited against future royalty payments or, at Greenfield\'s election, refunded by NovaTrait within thirty (30) days.

**5.4** **Confidentiality of Audit.** The Auditor shall be required to execute a confidentiality agreement reasonably acceptable to Greenfield prior to conducting any audit. The Auditor shall disclose to NovaTrait only its conclusions regarding the accuracy of the Royalty Reports and the amount of any overpayment or underpayment, without disclosing specific financial details of Greenfield\'s business.

**[ARTICLE VI --- INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY OWNERSHIP AND IMPROVEMENTS]{.underline}**

**6.1** **Ownership of Licensed IP.** As between the Parties, NovaTrait shall retain sole and exclusive ownership of all Licensed IP existing as of the Effective Date.

**6.2** **NovaTrait Improvements.** NovaTrait shall solely own all NovaTrait Improvements. NovaTrait hereby grants to Greenfield a non-exclusive license under NovaTrait Improvements to the extent such NovaTrait Improvements are necessary or useful for Greenfield\'s exercise of the license granted under Section 3.1, subject to the same terms and conditions (including royalty obligations) as apply to the Licensed IP under this Agreement.

**6.3** **Greenfield Improvements.** Greenfield shall solely own all Greenfield Improvements. Greenfield hereby grants to NovaTrait a non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide license under Greenfield Improvements, with the right to sublicense, at a commercially reasonable royalty rate to be negotiated in good faith by the Parties.

**6.4** **Joint Improvements.** Joint Improvements shall be jointly owned by the Parties. Each Party may exploit Joint Improvements without the consent of or an accounting to the other Party, subject to the other provisions of this Agreement. Neither Party shall grant an exclusive license under Joint Improvements to any Third Party without the prior written consent of the other Party.

**[ARTICLE VII --- BIOLOGICAL MATERIALS]{.underline}**

**7.1** **Delivery of Biological Materials.** NovaTrait shall deliver the Biological Materials identified in **Exhibit B** to Greenfield within sixty (60) days following the Effective Date, or at such other times as the Parties may agree in writing. NovaTrait shall deliver such Biological Materials in accordance with applicable shipping and handling requirements, including all applicable biosafety regulations.

**7.2** **Use of Biological Materials.** Greenfield shall use the Biological Materials solely for the purposes authorized under this Agreement and in accordance with all applicable laws, regulations, and guidelines, including without limitation all biosafety and environmental regulations. Greenfield shall not transfer, provide, or make available any Biological Materials to any Third Party without the prior written consent of NovaTrait, except to the extent necessary for an authorized Sublicensee to exercise the rights granted under a permitted sublicense.

**7.3** **Handling and Storage.** Greenfield shall handle, store, and maintain the Biological Materials in accordance with NovaTrait\'s written instructions and all applicable regulatory requirements. Greenfield shall maintain accurate records of the receipt, storage, use, and disposition of all Biological Materials.

**7.4** **Return or Destruction of Biological Materials.** Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason, Greenfield shall, at NovaTrait\'s election:

> \(a\) return to NovaTrait all Biological Materials (including all copies, progeny, and derivatives thereof) in Greenfield\'s possession or control; or
>
> \(b\) destroy all Biological Materials (including all copies, progeny, and derivatives thereof) in Greenfield\'s possession or control and provide NovaTrait with a written certificate of destruction executed by an authorized officer of Greenfield.

Greenfield shall complete the return or destruction of Biological Materials within ninety (90) days following the effective date of expiration or termination of this Agreement (the \"**Wind-Down Period**\"). During the Wind-Down Period, Greenfield may continue to use the Biological Materials solely to the extent necessary to fulfill existing contractual obligations to Third Parties for Licensed Products that are in the process of manufacture, production, or delivery as of the date of expiration or termination, provided that Greenfield shall pay all royalties due on any Licensed Products sold during the Wind-Down Period.

**7.5** **Survival of Biological Materials Obligations.** Greenfield\'s obligations under this Article VII with respect to the handling, return, or destruction of Biological Materials shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement.

**[ARTICLE VIII --- CONFIDENTIALITY]{.underline}**

**8.1** **Obligations.** During the Term and for a period of seven (7) years following the expiration or termination of this Agreement (or, with respect to trade secrets, for so long as such information remains a trade secret under applicable law), the Receiving Party shall: (a) hold the Disclosing Party\'s Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) not disclose such Confidential Information to any Third Party except as expressly permitted herein; and (c) not use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than the exercise of its rights or the performance of its obligations under this Agreement.

**8.2** **Exceptions.** The obligations set forth in Section 8.1 shall not apply to information that: (a) was in the public domain at the time of disclosure or enters the public domain thereafter through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) was known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party, as demonstrated by written records; (c) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to or use of the Disclosing Party\'s Confidential Information; or (d) is lawfully received by the Receiving Party from a Third Party without restriction on disclosure.

**8.3** **Permitted Disclosures.** The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party: (a) to its Affiliates, employees, agents, consultants, and contractors who have a need to know such information for purposes related to this Agreement and who are bound by written confidentiality obligations no less protective than those set forth herein; (b) to the extent required by applicable law, regulation, or order of a court or governmental authority of competent jurisdiction, provided that the Receiving Party shall, to the extent legally permitted, give the Disclosing Party prompt written notice of such required disclosure and cooperate with the Disclosing Party\'s efforts to obtain a protective order or other appropriate remedy; and (c) to actual or prospective investors, lenders, acquirers, or licensees in connection with due diligence or similar activities, provided that such persons are bound by written confidentiality obligations no less protective than those set forth herein.

**[ARTICLE IX --- REGULATORY MATTERS]{.underline}**

**9.1** **Regulatory Responsibilities.** Greenfield shall be solely responsible for obtaining and maintaining all regulatory approvals, permits, and registrations required for the development, manufacture, use, sale, and commercialization of Licensed Products within the Territory. NovaTrait shall provide reasonable cooperation and technical support to Greenfield in connection with such regulatory activities, at Greenfield\'s expense.

**9.2** **Compliance.** Each Party shall comply with all applicable laws, rules, and regulations in connection with its activities under this Agreement, including without limitation all applicable biosafety, environmental, import/export, and trade compliance laws and regulations.

**9.3** **Adverse Events and Recalls.** Greenfield shall promptly notify NovaTrait of any material adverse event, safety issue, or regulatory action relating to any Licensed Product. In the event that any Regulatory Authority requires or either Party reasonably determines that a recall or market withdrawal of any Licensed Product is necessary, the Parties shall cooperate in good faith to implement such recall or withdrawal in an orderly and expeditious manner.

**[ARTICLE X --- INDEMNIFICATION AND LIMITATION OF LIABILITY]{.underline}**

**10.1** **Indemnification by Greenfield.** Greenfield shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless NovaTrait and its Affiliates, and their respective directors, officers, employees, and agents (the \"**NovaTrait Indemnitees**\"), from and against any and all losses, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) arising out of or relating to any Third Party claim based on: (a) Greenfield\'s development, manufacture, use, sale, or commercialization of Licensed Products; (b) any breach by Greenfield of its representations, warranties, or obligations under this Agreement; or (c) the negligence or willful misconduct of Greenfield or its employees or agents; except, in each case, to the extent such losses arise from the negligence or willful misconduct of any NovaTrait Indemnitee or from a breach by NovaTrait of its representations, warranties, or obligations under this Agreement.

**10.2** **Indemnification by NovaTrait.** NovaTrait shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Greenfield and its Affiliates, and their respective directors, officers, employees, and agents (the \"**Greenfield Indemnitees**\"), from and against any and all losses, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) arising out of or relating to any Third Party claim based on: (a) any breach by NovaTrait of its representations, warranties, or obligations under this Agreement; (b) a claim that the Licensed IP as provided by NovaTrait infringes the intellectual property rights of a Third Party in the Territory; or (c) the negligence or willful misconduct of NovaTrait or its employees or agents; except, in each case, to the extent such losses arise from the negligence or willful misconduct of any Greenfield Indemnitee or from a breach by Greenfield of its representations, warranties, or obligations under this Agreement.

**10.3** **Indemnification Procedures.** The Party seeking indemnification (the \"**Indemnified Party**\") shall: (a) give prompt written notice of the claim to the indemnifying Party (the \"**Indemnifying Party**\"); (b) give the Indemnifying Party sole control of the defense and settlement of such claim (provided that the Indemnifying Party shall not settle any claim in a manner that imposes any obligation on, or that is adverse to the interests of, the Indemnified Party without the Indemnified Party\'s prior written consent, not to be unreasonably withheld); and (c) provide reasonable cooperation to the Indemnifying Party at the Indemnifying Party\'s expense.

**10.4** **Limitation of Liability.** EXCEPT FOR A PARTY\'S INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS UNDER SECTIONS 10.1 AND 10.2, BREACHES OF ARTICLE VIII (CONFIDENTIALITY), OR A PARTY\'S WILLFUL MISCONDUCT OR FRAUD, IN NO EVENT SHALL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER PARTY FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, PUNITIVE, OR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION LOST PROFITS, LOSS OF REVENUE, LOSS OF DATA, OR COST OF PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES, HOWEVER CAUSED AND UNDER ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), STRICT LIABILITY, OR OTHERWISE, EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

**[ARTICLE XI --- INSURANCE]{.underline}**

**11.1** **Insurance Requirements.** During the Term and for a period of three (3) years following the expiration or termination of this Agreement, Greenfield shall maintain, at its own expense, the following insurance coverages: (a) commercial general liability insurance with limits of not less than Five Million Dollars (\$5,000,000) per occurrence and Ten Million Dollars (\$10,000,000) in the aggregate; (b) product liability insurance with limits of not less than Ten Million Dollars (\$10,000,000) per occurrence and Twenty Million Dollars (\$20,000,000) in the aggregate; and (c) such other insurance as may be required by applicable law. Greenfield shall name NovaTrait as an additional insured on all such policies and shall provide NovaTrait with certificates of insurance upon request.

**[ARTICLE XII --- TERM AND TERMINATION]{.underline}**

**12.1** **Term.** This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of seven (7) years, unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Article XII (the \"**Term**\"). The Term shall expire on December 31, 2024.

**12.2** **Renewal.** The Parties may renew this Agreement for one or more additional periods upon mutually agreed terms. Either Party wishing to renew this Agreement shall provide written notice to the other Party not less than ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the then-current Term (i.e., by September 30, 2024, for the initial Term). The Parties shall thereafter negotiate the terms of renewal in good faith.

**12.3** **Termination for Material Breach.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the other Party if the other Party commits a material breach of this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within sixty (60) days following receipt of written notice specifying the nature of the breach in reasonable detail. If the breach is of a nature that cannot be cured within sixty (60) days, the breaching Party shall not be in default if it commences cure within such sixty (60)-day period and diligently pursues such cure to completion within a reasonable period not to exceed one hundred twenty (120) days.

**12.4** **Termination for Insolvency.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice to the other Party if the other Party: (a) becomes insolvent or generally fails to pay its debts as they become due; (b) files or has filed against it a petition in bankruptcy or for reorganization under any bankruptcy or insolvency law; (c) makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors; (d) has a receiver, trustee, or similar officer appointed for its business or property; or (e) is dissolved or liquidated.

**12.5** **Termination by Greenfield for Convenience.** Greenfield may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon one hundred eighty (180) days\' prior written notice to NovaTrait. In the event of termination under this Section 12.5, Greenfield shall pay all royalties accrued through the effective date of termination.

**12.6** **Standstill Provision.** In the event that the Parties are engaged in good-faith negotiations for the renewal of this Agreement at the time of the expiration of the Term (or any renewal term), the terms and conditions of this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for a period of up to one hundred eighty (180) days following such expiration (the \"**Standstill Period**\"), pending the completion of such negotiations or the earlier termination of negotiations by either Party upon thirty (30) days\' written notice to the other Party. During the Standstill Period: (a) all rights and obligations of the Parties under this Agreement shall continue in effect; (b) Greenfield shall continue to pay royalties in accordance with Article IV; and (c) either Party may terminate the Standstill Period at any time upon thirty (30) days\' written notice to the other Party. For the avoidance of doubt, the Standstill Period shall not constitute a renewal of this Agreement and shall not be construed to create any obligation on either Party to agree to renewal terms.

**12.7** **Effects of Termination.** Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason (including upon the expiration of any Standstill Period under Section 12.6):

> \(a\) all licenses and rights granted to Greenfield under this Agreement shall immediately terminate, subject to Section 12.8;
>
> \(b\) Greenfield shall cease all use of the Licensed IP, including the Biological Materials, subject to the Wind-Down Period set forth in Section 7.4;
>
> \(c\) Greenfield shall return or destroy all Biological Materials in accordance with Section 7.4;
>
> \(d\) Greenfield shall pay to NovaTrait all royalties and other amounts accrued but unpaid as of the effective date of termination within thirty (30) days of such date;
>
> \(e\) each Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information of the other Party, except to the extent retention is required by applicable law or regulation; and
>
> \(f\) NovaTrait shall have no further obligation to provide technical support or know-how to Greenfield.

**12.8** **Sell-Off Period.** Notwithstanding Section 12.7(a), upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, Greenfield shall have the right, for a period of one hundred eighty (180) days following the effective date of expiration or termination (or following the expiration of any Standstill Period), to sell, distribute, or otherwise dispose of any inventory of Licensed Products that was manufactured prior to the effective date of expiration or termination, subject to Greenfield\'s continued obligation to pay royalties on such sales in accordance with Article IV.

**[ARTICLE XIII --- INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY ENFORCEMENT]{.underline}**

**13.1** **Notification of Infringement.** Each Party shall promptly notify the other Party in writing upon becoming aware of any actual or suspected infringement of the Licensed Patents by a Third Party within the Field of Use and the Territory.

**13.2** **Enforcement by NovaTrait.** NovaTrait shall have the first right, but not the obligation, to bring and control any action or proceeding to enforce the Licensed Patents against Third Party infringers within the Territory, at NovaTrait\'s expense. Greenfield shall provide reasonable cooperation and assistance to NovaTrait in connection with any such enforcement action at NovaTrait\'s expense.

**13.3** **Enforcement by Greenfield.** If NovaTrait does not initiate an enforcement action within one hundred twenty (120) days of receiving written notice of infringement from Greenfield under Section 13.1, or if NovaTrait notifies Greenfield in writing that it does not intend to take action, Greenfield shall have the right, but not the obligation, to bring and control such enforcement action at Greenfield\'s expense. NovaTrait shall provide reasonable cooperation and assistance to Greenfield in connection with any such enforcement action.

**13.4** **Allocation of Recoveries.** Any monetary recovery obtained in connection with an enforcement action under this Article XIII shall be allocated as follows: first, to reimburse the Party bringing the action for its reasonable out-of-pocket costs and expenses (including attorneys\' fees) incurred in connection with such action; and second, the remaining balance, if any, shall be shared equally between the Parties.

**[ARTICLE XIV --- DISPUTE RESOLUTION]{.underline}**

**14.1** **Escalation.** In the event of any dispute, claim, or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement (a \"**Dispute**\"), the Parties shall first attempt to resolve the Dispute through good-faith negotiations between the senior executives of each Party designated for such purpose. Either Party may initiate such negotiations by providing written notice to the other Party, and such negotiations shall continue for a period of thirty (30) days (or such longer period as the Parties may agree).

**14.2** **Mediation.** If the Parties are unable to resolve a Dispute through negotiations under Section 14.1, either Party may submit the Dispute to non-binding mediation administered by JAMS in Boston, Massachusetts, in accordance with its then-current mediation rules. The Parties shall share equally the costs of mediation.

**14.3** **Litigation.** If the Dispute is not resolved through mediation within sixty (60) days of the submission to JAMS (or such longer period as the Parties may agree), either Party may initiate litigation in the courts identified in Section 15.7.

**[ARTICLE XV --- GENERAL PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**15.1** **Governing Law.** This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

**15.2** **Notices.** All notices, requests, demands, and other communications required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed duly given: (a) upon personal delivery; (b) one (1) business day after deposit with a nationally recognized overnight courier service, prepaid; (c) upon transmission by email (with confirmation of receipt); or (d) three (3) business days after deposit in the United States mail, postage prepaid, registered or certified, return receipt requested, addressed as follows:

If to NovaTrait:

> NovaTrait Genomics Corporation 77 Kendall Square, Building 6 Cambridge, MA 02142
>
> Attention: Chief Licensing Officer
>
> Email: licensing@novatrait.com

With a copy (which shall not constitute notice) to:

> Stonebridge Harmon LLP One Federal Street, 30th Floor Boston, MA 02110
>
> Attention: Graham Whitfield
>
> Email: gwhitfield@stonebridgeharmon.com

If to Greenfield:

> Greenfield Biosciences, Inc. 4200 Research Park Drive, Suite 310 Raleigh, NC 27609
>
> Attention: General Counsel
>
> Email: legal@greenfieldbiosciences.com

Either Party may change its address for notices by giving written notice to the other Party in accordance with this Section 15.2.

**15.3** **Entire Agreement.** This Agreement, together with all Exhibits attached hereto, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, relating to such subject matter. No amendment, modification, or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both Parties.

**15.4** **Severability.** If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable, and the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect.

**15.5** **Assignment.** Neither Party may assign or transfer this Agreement or any of its rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that either Party may assign this Agreement without the other Party\'s consent: (a) to an Affiliate of such Party; or (b) to a successor in connection with a merger, consolidation, or sale of all or substantially all of the assets of such Party to which this Agreement relates, provided that the assignee assumes in writing all of the assigning Party\'s obligations under this Agreement. Any attempted assignment in violation of this Section 15.5 shall be null and void.

**15.6** **Force Majeure.** Neither Party shall be liable for any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement (other than payment obligations) to the extent that such delay or failure is caused by a Force Majeure Event. \"**Force Majeure Event**\" means any event beyond the reasonable control of the affected Party, including without limitation natural disasters, acts of God, war, terrorism, epidemics or pandemics, government actions or orders, labor disputes, and failures of utilities or telecommunications.

**15.7** **Jurisdiction and Venue.** Each Party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in Suffolk County, Massachusetts, for the resolution of any Dispute not resolved through mediation under Section 14.2. Each Party waives any objection to the laying of venue in, and any claim of inconvenient forum with respect to, such courts.

**15.8** **Waiver.** No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the waiving Party. No failure or delay by either Party in exercising any right, power, or remedy under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise of any such right, power, or remedy preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or remedy.

**15.9** **Relationship of the Parties.** The relationship between the Parties is that of independent contractors. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create any agency, partnership, joint venture, or employment relationship between the Parties.

**15.10** **Counterparts.** This Agreement may be executed in two or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Execution and delivery of this Agreement by exchange of electronic copies bearing electronic signatures shall be deemed equivalent to execution and delivery of an original.

**15.11** **Construction.** This Agreement has been negotiated by the Parties and their respective counsel and shall be interpreted fairly in accordance with its terms, without any strict construction in favor of or against either Party. The headings and captions in this Agreement are for convenience of reference only and shall not affect the interpretation of this Agreement.

**15.12** **Third-Party Beneficiaries.** This Agreement is for the sole benefit of the Parties and their respective permitted successors and assigns. Nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, is intended to or shall confer upon any Third Party any legal or equitable right, benefit, or remedy of any nature whatsoever.

**15.13** **Cumulative Remedies.** Except as otherwise expressly provided herein, the rights and remedies of the Parties under this Agreement are cumulative and not exclusive of any rights or remedies available to such Party at law or in equity.

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF**, the Parties have caused this Agreement to be executed by their duly authorized representatives as of the Effective Date.

**Part 2 --- Know-How and Trade Secrets**

The Licensed IP includes the following categories of know-how and trade secrets, each as more particularly described in the confidential technical annexes delivered to Greenfield on or about the Effective Date:

1\. Proprietary protocols for the construction, assembly, and validation of expression vector systems for use in the Field of Use.

2\. Optimized transformation and regeneration protocols for corn, soybean, and cotton using the Biological Materials.

3\. Trait expression screening and selection methodologies, including marker-assisted selection protocols.

4\. Regulatory submission data packages and supporting documentation relating to the Licensed Patents and Biological Materials, to the extent NovaTrait is permitted to share such data under applicable law.

5\. Unpublished experimental data relating to trait expression efficiency, yield impact, and agronomic performance of Licensed Products developed using the Licensed IP.

**[EXHIBIT B --- BIOLOGICAL MATERIALS]{.underline}**

The following Biological Materials shall be delivered by NovaTrait to Greenfield in accordance with Section 7.1:

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  ----------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- ----------------------- ------------------------

  TF-BM-002         pTraitForge-DT2 Expression Vector (Drought Tolerance Cassette, Variant 2)   10 vials (50 µg each)   -80°C

  TF-BM-003         pTraitForge-NUE1 Expression Vector (Nutrient Uptake Efficiency Cassette)    10 vials (50 µg each)   -80°C

  TF-BM-004         Agrobacterium tumefaciens Strain AGL-TF (Transformation-competent)          5 glycerol stocks       -80°C

  TF-BM-006         Reference Seed Stock --- Soybean (Glycine max, Inbred Line TF-S1)           500 seeds               4°C, desiccated

  TF-BM-007         Reference Seed Stock --- Cotton (Gossypium hirsutum, Inbred Line TF-Ct1)    300 seeds               4°C, desiccated

All Biological Materials are provided subject to the terms of this Agreement and remain the property of NovaTrait as set forth in Section 6.1. Greenfield shall not modify the Biological Materials except as necessary for authorized use within the Field of Use and in accordance with the protocols described in Exhibit A, Part 2.

**[EXHIBIT C --- EXISTING ENCUMBRANCES ON LICENSED IP]{.underline}**

As of the Effective Date, the following encumbrances exist with respect to the Licensed IP:

1\. **Research Collaboration Agreement** --- NovaTrait and the Massachusetts Institute of Agricultural Sciences entered into a Research Collaboration Agreement dated September 14, 2015, pursuant to which certain foundational research underlying U.S. Patent Nos. 10,443,076 and 10,443,077 was jointly funded. Under the terms of such agreement, the Massachusetts Institute of Agricultural Sciences retains a non-exclusive, royalty-free license for non-commercial academic research purposes only. Such license does not include any right to sublicense, make commercial use of, or transfer the Licensed IP, and does not affect the rights granted to Greenfield under this Agreement.

2\. **Sponsored Research Agreement** --- NovaTrait and the Brazilian Agricultural Research Corporation (Embrapa) entered into a Sponsored Research Agreement dated February 1, 2017, pursuant to which Embrapa conducted field trials of expression vector constructs in soybean varieties in Brazil. Under the terms of such agreement, Embrapa retains certain rights to research data generated during such field trials for publication and non-commercial research purposes, subject to NovaTrait\'s prior review and approval. No rights to the Licensed Patents or Biological Materials were granted to Embrapa.

No other liens, encumbrances, security interests, or Third Party rights exist with respect to the Licensed IP as of the Effective Date.

**GENOMAP PRO SOFTWARE LICENSE AGREEMENT**

**THIS GENOMAP PRO SOFTWARE LICENSE AGREEMENT** (this \"**Agreement**\") is made and entered into as of March 1, 2020 (the \"**Effective Date**\"), by and between:

**NOVATRAIT GENOMICS CORPORATION**, a Massachusetts corporation, with its principal place of business at 77 Kendall Square, Building 6, Cambridge, MA 02142 (\"**Licensor**\" or \"**NovaTrait**\"),

and

**GREENFIELD BIOSCIENCES, INC.**, a Delaware corporation, with its principal place of business at 4200 Research Park Drive, Suite 310, Raleigh, NC 27609 (\"**Licensee**\" or \"**Greenfield**\").

Each of NovaTrait and Greenfield may be referred to herein individually as a \"**Party**\" and collectively as the \"**Parties**.\"

**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, NovaTrait has developed and owns a proprietary bioinformatics software platform known as \"GenoMap Pro\" (Version 4.x series), including associated source code, object code, documentation, algorithms, databases, and related intellectual property, which is used for genomic analysis, trait mapping, and agricultural product development support;

**WHEREAS**, Greenfield is engaged in the development and commercialization of genetically enhanced crop varieties and desires to obtain a license to use the GenoMap Pro platform in connection with its internal research, development, and product development activities;

**WHEREAS**, the Parties have entered into that certain Gene-Editing Platform License Agreement dated July 1, 2015 (the \"**CRISPR-Ag Suite License**\") and that certain Plant Trait Expression Platform License Agreement dated January 1, 2018 (the \"**TraitForge License**\"), and Greenfield utilizes the GenoMap Pro platform in conjunction with the technologies licensed under those agreements;

**WHEREAS**, the Parties desire to set forth the terms and conditions under which NovaTrait shall grant to Greenfield, and Greenfield shall accept, a license to use the GenoMap Pro platform, including access to the source code thereof for internal modification purposes, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement;

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants, agreements, representations, and warranties set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

**[ARTICLE I --- DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 1.1 --- Definitions.** As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:

\"**Accepted Use Policy**\" means the policy governing the acceptable use of the Software, as set forth in **Exhibit A** attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference, as may be amended from time to time by mutual written agreement of the Parties.

\"**Authorized Users**\" means employees, independent contractors, and consultants of Greenfield who are authorized by Greenfield to access and use the Software in accordance with the terms of this Agreement for purposes within the scope of the License.

\"**Confidential Information**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 10.1.

\"**Copyright Registration**\" means U.S. Copyright Registration No. TX-8-912-445, as filed with the United States Copyright Office.

\"**Derivative Data**\" means any data, analyses, results, reports, visualizations, or other output generated by the Software through the processing, analysis, transformation, or manipulation of Input Data or other data sources.

\"**Documentation**\" means all user manuals, technical specifications, release notes, training materials, application programming interface documentation, and other written materials provided by NovaTrait to Greenfield in connection with the Software, whether in printed or electronic form.

\"**Effective Date**\" has the meaning set forth in the preamble.

\"**Input Data**\" means any data, datasets, genomic sequences, biological information, experimental results, or other information that Greenfield inputs into, uploads to, or otherwise makes available to the Software for processing.

\"**Intellectual Property Rights**\" means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, know-how, moral rights, database rights, and all other intellectual property rights of any kind, whether registered or unregistered, and all applications, renewals, extensions, and restorations thereof, in any jurisdiction worldwide.

\"**License**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 2.1.

\"**License Fee**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 4.1.

\"**Major Version Upgrade**\" means a release of the Software that is designated by NovaTrait with a new primary version number (e.g., Version 5.x), as distinguished from a Minor Update.

\"**Minor Update**\" means a release of the Software that is designated by NovaTrait with an incremental version number within the same primary version series (e.g., Version 4.1, 4.2, 4.3), including bug fixes, security patches, and incremental feature enhancements.

\"**Object Code**\" means the machine-readable, compiled form of the Software.

\"**Renewal Term**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 3.2.

\"**Software**\" means the GenoMap Pro bioinformatics software platform, Version 4.x series (including all Minor Updates provided during the Term), in both Source Code and Object Code form, together with all associated Documentation.

\"**Source Code**\" means the human-readable form of the Software, including all comments, annotations, and programmer notes, together with all compilation and build scripts, libraries, and tools necessary to compile, build, and generate the Object Code from the Source Code.

\"**Source Code Escrow**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 5.3.

\"**Term**\" has the meaning set forth in Section 3.1.

\"**Territory**\" means worldwide.

**[ARTICLE II --- GRANT OF LICENSE]{.underline}**

**Section 2.1 --- License Grant.** Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and Greenfield\'s payment of the License Fee, NovaTrait hereby grants to Greenfield a non-exclusive, non-transferable (except as set forth in Section 13.4), limited license (the \"**License**\") to install, access, and use the Software, including the Source Code and Object Code, within the Territory, solely for Greenfield\'s internal business purposes in connection with genomic analysis, trait mapping, and product development support related to Greenfield\'s agricultural biotechnology programs.

**Section 2.2 --- Scope of Use.** The License granted pursuant to Section 2.1 permits Greenfield to:

> \(a\) install and operate the Software on Greenfield\'s owned or controlled computing infrastructure, including on-premises servers and Greenfield-controlled cloud computing environments;
>
> \(b\) permit Authorized Users to access and use the Software for purposes within the scope of the License;
>
> \(c\) access, review, and internally modify the Source Code solely for purposes of adapting, configuring, or enhancing the Software for Greenfield\'s internal use, subject to Section 5.2;
>
> \(d\) make a reasonable number of archival and backup copies of the Software; and
>
> \(e\) use the Documentation in connection with Greenfield\'s authorized use of the Software.

**Section 2.3 --- Restrictions on Use.** Except as expressly permitted by this Agreement, Greenfield shall not, and shall not permit any third party to:

> \(a\) sublicense, distribute, sell, lease, rent, loan, or otherwise make the Software available to any third party;
>
> \(b\) use the Software for any purpose other than Greenfield\'s internal business purposes as described in Section 2.1;
>
> \(c\) commercially redistribute the Software, or any portion thereof, in any form, whether as a standalone product or incorporated into any other product or service;
>
> \(d\) reverse engineer, decompile, or disassemble the Object Code of the Software, except to the extent that such activity is expressly permitted by applicable law notwithstanding this limitation;
>
> \(e\) remove, alter, or obscure any proprietary notices, copyright notices, or other intellectual property markings on or in the Software or Documentation; or
>
> \(f\) use the Software to provide services to third parties on a service bureau, time-sharing, or application service provider basis.

**Section 2.4 --- No Sublicensing.** The License is personal to Greenfield and may not be sublicensed in whole or in part. For the avoidance of doubt, Greenfield may not grant any third party any rights to access or use the Software, except through the engagement of Authorized Users in accordance with this Agreement.

**Section 2.5 --- Reservation of Rights.** All rights not expressly granted to Greenfield under this Agreement are reserved by NovaTrait. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting to Greenfield any ownership interest in the Software or any Intellectual Property Rights therein.

**[ARTICLE III --- TERM AND RENEWAL]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 --- Initial Term.** This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated in accordance with Article XI, shall continue in effect for a period of five (5) years, expiring on February 28, 2025 (the \"**Term**\").

**Section 3.2 --- Renewal.** This Agreement may be renewed for one or more successive renewal terms (each, a \"**Renewal Term**\") upon the mutual written agreement of the Parties. Either Party desiring to renew this Agreement shall provide written notice to the other Party no later than ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the then-current Term or Renewal Term (as applicable). For the avoidance of doubt, the initial Term shall expire on February 28, 2025, and accordingly, any notice of intent to renew the initial Term must be delivered on or before November 30, 2024. The License Fee, and any other terms applicable to a Renewal Term, shall be as mutually agreed by the Parties in writing prior to the commencement of such Renewal Term. Absent such mutual written agreement, this Agreement shall expire at the end of the then-current Term or Renewal Term.

**Section 3.3 --- Effect of Expiration.** Upon expiration of the Term or any Renewal Term without renewal, Greenfield\'s rights under this Agreement shall terminate, and Greenfield shall comply with the obligations set forth in Section 11.4 (Effects of Termination). For the avoidance of doubt, the expiration of this Agreement shall not affect the survival of any provisions that by their nature are intended to survive, as set forth in Section 11.5.

**[ARTICLE IV --- LICENSE FEE AND PAYMENT]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 --- License Fee.** In consideration of the License granted hereunder, Greenfield shall pay to NovaTrait a flat annual license fee of Three Million One Hundred Forty Thousand Dollars (\$3,140,000.00) (the \"**License Fee**\") for each year of the Term. The License Fee shall not be subject to any percentage-of-sales, per-unit, or usage-based royalty calculations.

**Section 4.2 --- Payment Schedule.** The License Fee shall be payable in four (4) equal quarterly installments of Seven Hundred Eighty-Five Thousand Dollars (\$785,000.00) each, due and payable on the first business day of each calendar quarter during the Term (i.e., on or about March 1, June 1, September 1, and December 1 of each year). The first quarterly installment shall be due on the Effective Date.

**Section 4.3 --- Late Payments.** Any License Fee payment not received by NovaTrait within thirty (30) days after its due date shall bear interest at the lesser of (a) one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month, and (b) the maximum rate permitted by applicable law, calculated from the due date until the date of actual payment.

**Section 4.4 --- Taxes.** All License Fees are exclusive of any sales, use, withholding, value-added, or other taxes, duties, or governmental charges imposed on or in connection with the transactions contemplated by this Agreement (\"**Taxes**\"). Greenfield shall be responsible for all such Taxes, excluding taxes based on NovaTrait\'s net income. If Greenfield is required by applicable law to deduct or withhold any Taxes from amounts payable to NovaTrait, Greenfield shall gross up such payment so that NovaTrait receives the full amount of the License Fee without reduction.

**Section 4.5 --- No Refunds.** Except as expressly provided in Section 7.3 (Warranty Remedy) or Section 11.4(c) (Pro-Rata Refund Upon Termination for Licensor\'s Breach), all License Fees paid are non-refundable.

**Section 4.6 --- Records and Audit.** Greenfield shall maintain accurate books and records relating to its use of the Software and payment of License Fees. NovaTrait shall have the right, upon not less than thirty (30) calendar days\' prior written notice and no more than once per calendar year, to audit Greenfield\'s books and records as they relate to Greenfield\'s compliance with this Agreement, including verification that Greenfield\'s use of the Software is within the scope of the License. Such audit shall be conducted during Greenfield\'s normal business hours at Greenfield\'s principal place of business by an independent certified public accountant selected by NovaTrait and reasonably acceptable to Greenfield. The cost of such audit shall be borne by NovaTrait unless the audit reveals a material breach of this Agreement by Greenfield, in which case the reasonable costs of the audit shall be borne by Greenfield.

**[ARTICLE V --- SOURCE CODE; MODIFICATIONS; ESCROW]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 --- Delivery of Source Code.** Within thirty (30) days after the Effective Date, NovaTrait shall deliver to Greenfield a complete copy of the Source Code of the Software (Version 4.0) in a commonly used electronic format, together with all compilation and build scripts, libraries, and tools necessary to compile, build, and generate the Object Code from the Source Code. NovaTrait shall deliver updated Source Code for each Minor Update within thirty (30) days of the commercial release of such Minor Update.

**Section 5.2 --- Internal Modification Rights.** Greenfield shall have the right to access, review, and internally modify the Source Code solely for the purpose of adapting, configuring, or enhancing the Software for Greenfield\'s internal use within the scope of the License. Any modifications, enhancements, adaptations, or derivative works created by Greenfield based on the Source Code (collectively, \"**Licensee Modifications**\") shall be subject to the following:

> \(a\) *Ownership.* All Licensee Modifications shall be owned by Greenfield; *provided, however*, that the underlying Source Code upon which such Licensee Modifications are based, and all Intellectual Property Rights therein, shall remain the sole and exclusive property of NovaTrait.
>
> \(b\) *License Back.* Greenfield hereby grants to NovaTrait a non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide license to use, reproduce, modify, and create derivative works of the Licensee Modifications, solely for NovaTrait\'s internal purposes and for incorporation into future versions of the Software for NovaTrait\'s other licensees.
>
> \(c\) *No Distribution.* Greenfield shall not distribute, disclose, or make available any Licensee Modifications to any third party without NovaTrait\'s prior written consent.
>
> \(d\) *Support Limitations.* NovaTrait shall have no obligation to provide support, maintenance, or warranty coverage for any Licensee Modifications or for any Software that has been modified by Greenfield to the extent that a reported issue is caused by or related to a Licensee Modification.
>
> \(e\) *Documentation of Modifications.* Greenfield shall maintain reasonable records of all Licensee Modifications, including a description of each modification, the date on which it was made, and the identity of the individual(s) who performed the modification.

**Section 5.3 --- Source Code Escrow.** Within sixty (60) days of the Effective Date, the Parties shall enter into a source code escrow agreement with an independent, nationally recognized escrow agent mutually acceptable to both Parties (the \"**Source Code Escrow**\"), pursuant to which NovaTrait shall deposit the Source Code of the Software, together with all compilation and build scripts, libraries, and tools necessary to compile, build, and generate the Object Code. NovaTrait shall update the escrow deposit with the Source Code for each Minor Update within thirty (30) days of its release. The Source Code Escrow shall provide for release of the escrowed materials to Greenfield upon the occurrence of any of the following release conditions:

> \(a\) NovaTrait files a petition for bankruptcy protection, or an involuntary bankruptcy petition is filed against NovaTrait and not dismissed within sixty (60) days;
>
> \(b\) NovaTrait makes a general assignment for the benefit of creditors;
>
> \(c\) NovaTrait ceases to conduct business operations in the ordinary course;
>
> \(d\) NovaTrait materially breaches its support and maintenance obligations under Article VIII and fails to cure such breach within sixty (60) days after written notice from Greenfield; or
>
> \(e\) NovaTrait is acquired by, or merged into, an entity that is a direct competitor of Greenfield, and the surviving entity fails to provide written assurance of continued performance under this Agreement within thirty (30) days of the closing of such transaction.

**Section 5.4 --- Source Code Security.** Greenfield shall implement and maintain commercially reasonable technical and organizational security measures to protect the confidentiality and integrity of the Source Code at all times, including the measures set forth in Section 10.3. Access to the Source Code shall be limited to Greenfield personnel who have a demonstrable need-to-know for purposes consistent with the License and who are bound by written confidentiality obligations no less protective than those set forth in Article X.

**[ARTICLE VI --- UPDATES AND UPGRADES]{.underline}**

**Section 6.1 --- Minor Updates.** During the Term, NovaTrait shall provide Greenfield with all Minor Updates to the Software at no additional charge. NovaTrait shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide each Minor Update to Greenfield within thirty (30) days of its general commercial release. Each Minor Update shall be deemed part of the Software and shall be subject to all terms and conditions of this Agreement.

**Section 6.2 --- Major Version Upgrades.** Major Version Upgrades (i.e., releases designated with a new primary version number, such as Version 5.x) are not included within the scope of this Agreement and shall not be provided to Greenfield under this License. Access to any Major Version Upgrade shall require a separate written agreement between the Parties, including agreement on applicable license fees, terms, and conditions. NovaTrait shall have no obligation to develop or release any Major Version Upgrade, nor to make any Major Version Upgrade available to Greenfield on any particular timeline or at any particular price.

**Section 6.3 --- Continued Support for Version 4.x.** Notwithstanding the release of any Major Version Upgrade, NovaTrait shall continue to provide support and maintenance for the Version 4.x series of the Software in accordance with Article VIII for the duration of the Term (including any Renewal Term). NovaTrait shall not discontinue support for the Version 4.x series during the Term without Greenfield\'s prior written consent.

**Section 6.4 --- Backward Compatibility.** NovaTrait shall use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that all Minor Updates to the Software are backward compatible with Greenfield\'s existing data formats, configurations, and Licensee Modifications. In the event that a Minor Update is not fully backward compatible, NovaTrait shall provide Greenfield with reasonable advance notice and a commercially reasonable migration or conversion path.

**[ARTICLE VIII --- SUPPORT AND MAINTENANCE]{.underline}**

**Section 8.1 --- Support Services.** During the Term, NovaTrait shall provide Greenfield with technical support for the Software during NovaTrait\'s standard business hours (Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Eastern Time, excluding NovaTrait-observed holidays), including:

>
> \(c\) access to NovaTrait\'s online knowledge base and support portal; and
>
> \(d\) remote diagnostic support upon Greenfield\'s request and authorization.

  ----------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------
  Level 1 (Critical)      Software is inoperable or a critical function is unavailable, with no workaround   4 business hours

**Section 8.3 --- Maintenance.** NovaTrait shall provide commercially reasonable maintenance for the Software during the Term, including the provision of Minor Updates in accordance with Section 6.1 and the correction of material defects in a commercially reasonable timeframe.

**Section 8.4 --- Excluded Services.** NovaTrait\'s support and maintenance obligations do not extend to: (a) issues caused by Licensee Modifications, unless NovaTrait can reproduce the issue in an unmodified version of the Software; (b) issues caused by Greenfield\'s use of the Software in a manner inconsistent with the Documentation or the Accepted Use Policy; (c) issues caused by hardware or software not provided by NovaTrait; or (d) training services, which shall be provided as mutually agreed at NovaTrait\'s then-current training rates.
